decisions.rationale: Dependency set (react 18.3, react-redux 9, @reduxjs/toolkit 2, RTL 14 as devDeps for the dual-target harness alias) landed with module 1 instead of last as planned: connect()->hooks conversions cannot execute against react-redux 5, so every converted component must be tested on the modern runtime immediately.

store.js: RTK configureStore replaces deprecated createStore+combineReducers, but action creators and switch reducers stay hand-written verbatim - createSlice/createAction can only carry data under `payload`, and the pinned public action shape {type:'ADD_TODO', text} has a top-level text field.

store.js: RTK default thunk middleware disabled (middleware: gDM => gDM({thunk:false})) - the legacy store accepted plain-object actions only (dispatching a function threw); enabling thunk would silently extend the public dispatch contract.

Counter/TodoList: dropping connect() removes the ownProps override surface (count, onIncrement, onDecrement, todos, onAddTodo are no longer injectable props) - observable API narrowing per plan risk 3; no consumer in this repo passes them.
TodoList.js: preserved verbatim per plan flags - no-trim empty-string submit guard (whitespace-only and '0' todos ARE added), dispatch-then-clear submit order, index-as-key list rendering.

Harness accommodation (only test change): Counter.test.js external-dispatch case wraps store.dispatch in act() - React 18 batches store updates originating outside React into a microtask where React 16 flushed synchronously; the pinned behavior is 'view reflects the dispatch once processed', not the legacy flush timing. Modified suite re-verified 27/27 green against original/.

testGates.summary: Tests 27 passed (27) — full suite vs migrated/ (react 18.3 / react-redux 9 / RTK 2)
Tests 27 passed (27) — identical suite (incl. act() accommodation) vs original/
